content.text:
You are classifying files for an AI-first external drive.

For each file object in the input JSON array, return a JSON array with one object per file.
Return valid JSON only. No markdown. No prose.

Each output object must contain:
- rel_path
- title
- content_kind
- domain
- lifecycle
- confidence
- tags: array of short strings
- summary: one sentence
- entities: array of objects with fields name and type
- relations: array of objects with fields from, relation, to
- recommended_zone
- canonical_priority: one of low, medium, high

Guidance:
- Infer semantics from path, filename, extension, and sampled text.
- Use practical domains like ai, security, infra, career, health, research, finance, media, prompt_engineering.
- Use lifecycle values only from: inbox, active, reference, archive, system.
- Keep entities and relations conservative; do not invent specifics unsupported by the input.
- Favor retrieval usefulness over literary summaries.
- Use recommended_zone only from: 00_inbox, 10_active, 20_reference, 30_archive, 40_system.
- Prefer 20_reference for documents meant to be reused, 10_active for live workspaces, 30_archive for dated snapshots and exports, 40_system for manifests/config/state, 00_inbox for ambiguous intake.

Input JSON:
[{"rel_path": "20_reference/_index.json", "name": "_index.json", "extension": ".json", "mime_type": "application/json", "size_bytes": 614, "zone": "20_reference", "sample_text": "{\n  \"title\": \"Reference Zone\",\n  \"purpose\": \"Stable reusable knowledge, documents, manuals, prompts, datasets, and long-lived support material.\",\n  \"status\": \"active\",\n  \"zone\": \"20_reference\",\n  \"tags\": [\"reference\", \"knowledge\", \"documents\", \"reuse\"],\n  \"related_paths\": [\n    \"20_reference/knowledge_base\",\n    \"20_reference/playbooks\",\n    \"20_reference/resumes\",\n    \"20_reference/manuals\",\n    \"20_reference/prompts\",\n    \"20_reference/datasets\",\n    \"20_reference/assets\"\n  ],\n  \"ai_summary\": \"Primary knowledge zone for retrieval-augmented use; prioritize semantic summaries and entity extraction here.\"\n}"}]

thoughts.subject: Beginning Classification Task
thoughts.description: I'm focused on parsing the JSON file now. My current objective is to ensure the output adheres precisely to the user's requested structure. Right now, I'm defining the necessary fields: `rel_path`, `title`, `content_kind`, `domain`, `lifecycle`, `confidence`, `tags`, `summary`, `entities`, `relations`, `recommended_zone`, and `canonical_priority`. I need to correctly extract and map the data for each of these fields.

thoughts.subject: Inferring Initial Attributes
thoughts.description: Now I'm diving deeper into the specifics, using the initial data. I've successfully extracted and assigned values to `rel_path` and am working on `title`, tentatively setting it to "Reference Zone Index". My focus is now on determining `content_kind`, `domain`, `lifecycle`, and `confidence`. Based on the context, I think `content_kind` will be "metadata," and `lifecycle` is likely "system". I'll use "high" for confidence given the explicit nature of the data. I'm now cross-referencing this against my knowledge base.

thoughts.subject: Refining Attribute Mapping
thoughts.description: I'm solidifying the values for each field now. I've pinned down `content_kind` to "configuration", with a potential secondary tag of "metadata". For `domain`, I've expanded to include "ai", "research", and "software development." I have the lifecycle set to "system" and confidence to "high." The tags are now refined, as well. Now I'm preparing the summary and will then move on to the more complex elements.

thoughts.subject: Generating Entity and Relation Data
thoughts.description: I've determined all of the attributes I need, and now I'm constructing the 'entities' and 'relations' fields. I'm extracting key concepts such as "Reference Zone," "knowledge," "documents," "prompts," and "datasets." I'm also defining the relationships between them. For the "entities" field, I'm setting the `name` and `type`. For the relations, I'm defining the `from`, `relation`, and `to` attributes. This step should wrap up the core data extraction.
